title: Explain Open Container Initiative (OCI) acronym, add link

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

@ElijahLynn thanks for the contribution!  I don't think you signed your commit and the test will likely fail due to that.  You'll need to `git commit --amend -s` and then repush.
Nice catch btb, I would sworn we'd a link on the page, but it must of inadvertently been removed at some point.  So thanks!

Looks like the web-UI signed the commit for me even though I didn't have a GPG/PGP key when I created it (I do now but it is a different key).
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1504756/44289485-72101d00-a242-11e8-984c-ee5e1c84275d.png)

Is the failure because of the signing? I looked briefly in the Red Hat CI test https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/1c1d5d9c98e32648fbe7482b744aca287ac1de46.0.1534540261773368490/output.log but couldn't tell.

Hi @ElijahLynn,

Yep, the tests are failing due to the PR not being signed.  I find it easier to spot in Travis and it shows:

```
HEAD is now at 2c632d1a Merge pull request #1824 from cyphar/fix-mips-build-devNumber
ln -sf ../../opencontainers/runc/runc
 1c1d5d9 - FAIL - does not have a valid DCO
1 commits to fix
make: *** [validate] Error 1
```

@ElijahLynn - `verified signature` has nothing to do with `signed-off`. 
The CI bot needs to see "Signed-off-by: Elijah Lynn <youremailhere>" in your commit body.

Make sure you set name and email in your `git config`:

```
git config user.name
git config user.email
```

If you don't see anything in the output set it:

```
git config --global user.name "Your Full Name"
git config --global user.name "your@email.com"
```

@rhatdan CI is failing because of missing signed off

```
ee4fe97 - FAIL - does not have a valid DCO
1 commits to fix
make: *** [validate] Error 1
```
We should be able to handle this, when someone corrects stuff via github, without forcing them to create a PR via git command line tool.
Thanks all, had no idea signed off and signed commits were different.

Related PR to better expose "Sign your PRs" in contributing.md TOC, https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/954. Will admit I did _not_ read contributing.md before this PR though, so it isn't like I missed it because of that. `#TIL`

:pushpin: Commit 6e4ac22 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 6e4ac228b44bfd71f9151d169c9ee498a27dbaa2 -->

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/c7676226362fd432e1d5e015e2388f68d383e065.1534972556868664576/index.html)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing c7676226362fd432e1d5e015e2388f68d383e065 to master...
@ElijahLynn thanks for sticking with this and pushing through o this PR.
